articles.title: IRGC arrests two suspects accused of aiding Israel, Tasnim reports
articles.summary: IRGC arrests two suspects accused of aiding Israel, Tasnim reports Iran’s Tasnim News Agency reported that the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps arrested two people in Urmia accused of collaborating with Israel. According to the report, the suspects allegedly shared coordinates and addresses of locations, including schools, mosques and Basij facilities through Telegram. Tasnim said the two also photographed sites after they were bombed and sent images of the damage to what it described as the “Zionist enemy”.

Video: Israeli strike hits crowded market in Gaza City
articles.summary: Video: Israeli strike hits crowded market in Gaza City An Israeli attack struck a group of civilians at Firas Market in eastern Gaza City, sending thick smoke into the air and triggering scenes of panic as people rushed to help the wounded. Video from the scene shows civilians running through the market as injured people are carried away following the strike. The attack is part of ongoing Israeli attacks across Gaza that have repeatedly hit civilian areas amid Israel's genocide in the enclave. The proposal, known as the “United States-Israel Defense Technology Cooperation Initiative”, is included as Section 224 of the House Armed Services Committee’s version of the fiscal year 2027 National Defense Authorization Act. articles.title: Axios: Trump seeks changes to proposed Iran agreement
articles.summary: Axios: Trump seeks changes to proposed Iran agreement US President Donald Trump has asked for “several amendments” to a proposed agreement negotiated by his envoys with Iran, according to Axios, citing US officials. The requested changes reportedly emerged during a White House Situation Room meeting on Friday. According to Axios, Trump wants revisions to clauses related to Iran’s nuclear programme and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. “It’s more specifics about how the US gets the material [Iran’s enriched uranium stockpile] and the timing,” a senior administration official told the outle

articles.title: IRGC: US drone conducting ‘hostile operation’ shot down
articles.summary: IRGC: US drone conducting ‘hostile operation’ shot down The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it shot down a US MQ-1 drone after it entered Iranian territorial waters and allegedly attempted to conduct a “hostile operation”. In a statement carried by Iranian state broadcaster IRIB, the IRGC said the drone “attempted to carry out a hostile operation” and “was immediately detected and targeted by the Revolutionary Guard’s modern defence missiles and shot down”. The IRGC added that “any aggression will be dealt with decisively.”

articles.title: We shouldn’t have been in Iran, Trump tells Fox News

We did so bad. It was such a foolish thing what we did. We shouldn’t have been there in the first place, by the way,” Trump said, before adding, “We shouldn’t have been in Iran, but Iran has the capability.” Trump claimed that without US strikes, “they would have a nuclear weapon right now and will be a whole different story,” adding, “If

articles.summary: Trump: We are ‘close to a very good agreement’ with Iran US President Donald Trump said Washington is “close to a very good agreement with Iran” but warned that military options remain available if negotiations fail. Speaking to Fox News, Trump said, “I prefer the diplomatic option because signing an agreement means immediately reopening the Strait of Hormuz to navigation,” adding that “the only and fundamental guarantee I adhere to is preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons.” Trump claimed that “the Iranians have already agreed not to develop or purchase a nuclear weapon” and said, “Th

articles.summary: Draft US-Iran deal would reopen Hormuz and ease sanctions, US media says US President Donald Trump met advisers on Friday to discuss extending the ceasefire with Iran but did not make a final decision, according to reports in the US media.  The meeting came amid claims that Washington and Tehran have reached a preliminary memorandum of understanding aimed at ending the conflict. articles.title: Iran unveils cruise missile-equipped high-speed naval attack craft in Tehran
articles.summary: Iran unveils cruise missile-equipped high-speed naval attack craft in Tehran Iran’s semi-official Fars news agency reported that the country’s military has unveiled a new naval attack craft named “27 Rajab” during a ceremony in Tehran’s Enqelab Square. According to the agency, the vessel is capable of reaching speeds of up to 100 knots (185 kilometres per hour) and is equipped to launch long-range cruise missiles. Fars described the deployment of the craft as a new symbol of Iran’s maritime military capabilities.

articles.title: Key issues unresolved in Iran-US talks, Tehran says
articles.summary: Key issues unresolved in Iran-US talks, Tehran says Iran says a proposed agreement with the United States has not yet been finalised and that negotiations remain ongoing through Pakistani mediators and other regional actors, despite signals from Washington that a deal may be close. Iranian officials say several major issues still need to be resolved before any formal announcement can be made. Posting on Instagram, Ben-Gvir said he had opposed the previous ceasefire with Lebanon and urged stronger military action against Beirut’s southern suburbs, an area where Hezbollah has a significant presence. articles.summary: Israeli raids and settler attacks intensify across occupied West Bank Israeli forces continued military operations across Gaza despite the ceasefire as Palestinians in the occupied West Bank reported a series of Israeli raids and attacks by Jewish settlers, according to the Palestinian news agency Wafa. articles.summary: Hezbollah says it carried out 24 attacks on Israeli troops and military sites Hezbollah said it carried out 24 military operations on Saturday targeting Israeli troops, military vehicles and infrastructure, while also launching multiple rocket barrages at the northern Israeli settlement of Kiryat Shmona.
